description: loki-vl-proxy operations: SLO/SLI health, Client→Proxy→VL operational view, deep proxy internals for tuning.

panels.title: Circuit Breaker
panels.description: 0 = closed (healthy), 1 = open (backend down)

panels.title: Request Rate by Status
panels.description: Request rate per second split by HTTP status class. Watch for 5xx growth.

panels.title: Error Rate %
panels.description: % of requests resulting in 4xx or 5xx responses.

panels.title: Client Latency Percentiles
panels.description: End-to-end client latency as seen by Grafana / API clients.

panels.title: Backend Latency Percentiles
panels.description: Latency for VictoriaLogs backend calls. Separates proxy overhead from backend pressure.

panels.title: Translation Errors
panels.description: LogQL→LogsQL translation success and error rates. Errors mean a client query could not be translated.

panels.title: Client Request Rate by Route
panels.description: Request rate broken down by Loki API route (query_range, labels, tail, etc.).

panels.title: Client Errors by Reason
panels.description: Client-attributed errors by reason (bad_request, rate_limited, etc.).

panels.title: Client Query Length (chars)
panels.description: Query character length distribution. Very long queries signal complex pipeline stages.

panels.title: Client Latency by Route (P99)
panels.description: P99 client latency per route. Identify which endpoints are slow for clients.

panels.title: Inflight Requests per Client
panels.description: Active in-flight requests per client. Helps identify heavy consumers.

panels.title: Request Coalescing (Saved Calls)
panels.description: Duplicate in-flight requests collapsed into one backend call. Saved = backend calls avoided.

panels.title: Internal Operations by Type
panels.description: Proxy-internal operation breakdown (translate_query, cache_hit, etc.) with outcome.

panels.title: Internal Operation Latency (P99)
panels.description: P99 latency for internal proxy operations. High translate_query latency = complex LogQL.

panels.title: Response Tuple Mode
panels.description: 2-tuple (default Loki) vs 3-tuple (structured metadata, when categorize-labels requested). Unexpected 3-tuple spikes may indicate client config drift.

panels.title: Tenant Request Rate
panels.description: Request rate per tenant (X-Scope-OrgID). Identify dominant tenants for capacity planning.

panels.title: Upstream Calls per Request (Fanout)
panels.description: Backend calls per client request. >1 = window fanout active. High p99 = large time ranges.

panels.title: Window Count Distribution
panels.description: Number of 1h windows per query_range request. High counts = clients querying large time ranges.

panels.title: Backend Duration by Endpoint
panels.description: P95 backend latency split by VL endpoint (select_logsql_query, hits, etc.).

panels.title: Window Fetch Latency
panels.description: Per-window fetch latency (backend round-trip). Use with adaptive parallelism panels.

panels.title: Window Merge Latency
panels.description: Time to merge window results into final Loki response. High = many windows or large responses.

panels.title: Adaptive Parallelism
panels.description: Current adaptive window parallelism (2–8 by default). EWMA latency/error drives adjustments.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Proxy process CPU usage ratio (0–1). user = LogQL work, system = OS syscalls.

panels.title: Memory
panels.description: RSS = OS view. Heap in-use = Go allocator view. Large gap = GC pressure or cache overhead.

panels.title: Goroutines & GC
panels.description: Goroutine count correlates with active window-fanout concurrency. GC cycles indicate memory pressure.

panels.title: Network I/O
panels.description: Network bytes per second. High rx = large backend responses. High tx = large client responses (compression helps).

panels.title: Disk I/O (L2 Cache)
panels.description: Disk read/write from L2 disk cache (bbolt). Only active when -disk-cache-path is set.

panels.title: System Pressure
panels.description: Linux PSI (pressure stall info). Non-zero = host resource contention affecting the proxy.

panels.title: Cache Hit Rate by Tier
panels.description: Cache hits per tier per second. High T0 = repeated identical requests served at edge. High L1/L2 = window reuse active.

panels.title: Cache Miss Rate by Tier
panels.description: Cache misses per tier — each miss cascades to the next tier or backend.

panels.title: Cache Size (Objects & Bytes)
panels.description: In-memory and disk cache utilization. Near -cache-max or -cache-max-bytes = eviction pressure.

panels.title: Cache Stale Hits & Backend Fallthrough
panels.description: Stale hits = TTL expired but served. Backend fallthrough = compat cache missed, went to L1+.

panels.title: Peer Cache Hit/Miss
panels.description: Peer cache activity. Errors = network problems with peer nodes.

panels.title: Write-Through Pushes
panels.description: Writes pushed from non-owner to owner peers. High push errors = peer network issues.

panels.title: Hot Read-Ahead Prefetching
panels.description: Proactive peer hot-key prefetching. Budget drops = byte/key limits enforced. Tenant skips = fairness applied.

panels.title: Peer Error Breakdown
panels.description: Peer cache errors broken down by reason (timeout, connection_refused, etc.).

panels.title: Window Cache Hit vs Miss
panels.description: Per-window cache efficiency. High hit rate = historical windows reused from L1/L2/L3.

panels.title: Prefilter Efficiency (Skipped Windows)
panels.description: Windows skipped by /hits index prefilter = VL queries avoided. High skip ratio = good prefilter ROI.

panels.title: Window Retries & Partial Responses
panels.description: Retries = backend transient errors. Partial responses = returned despite some window failures. Degraded batches = stream-aware throttling.

panels.title: Prefilter Duration
panels.description: Time spent on prefilter /hits calls. Should be fast (<50ms p99). Slow prefilter = skip it with fewer windows threshold.

panels.title: Adaptive Parallelism Over Time
panels.description: Adaptive parallelism trace. Drops = backend latency/error backpressure applied. Increases = backend recovered.

panels.title: Pattern Mining Rate
panels.description: Pattern mining pipeline. Pre > post = merge deduplication active. Stored = net new patterns.

panels.title: Source Lines Observed vs Scanned
panels.description: Pattern mining input pipeline. Scanned < observed = sampling active.

panels.title: Snapshot Hits & Reuse
panels.description: Pattern snapshot cache efficiency. High reuse = pattern computation avoided.

panels.title: Persistence (Disk & Peer)
panels.description: Pattern snapshot persistence. Restoration bytes = how much pattern state survived restarts.

panels.title: Connections by State
panels.description: Active HTTP connections by state (new, active, idle, hijacked). High idle = keepalive working.

panels.title: Connection Rotations by Reason
panels.description: Connection rotation reasons (max_age, max_requests, overload). Overload = backpressure shedding active.

panels.title: Connection Transitions
panels.description: Connection lifecycle state machine transitions. Rapid transitions = short-lived connection pressure.

panels.title: Per-Tenant Request Rate
panels.description: Request rate per X-Scope-OrgID tenant. Identify dominant tenants.

panels.title: Per-Tenant P99 Latency
panels.description: P99 latency by tenant. Noisy-tenant isolation: one tenant's slow queries don't explain another's.

panels.title: Per-Tenant Error Rate
panels.description: Error rate per tenant. Tenant-scoped backend failures or bad queries.

title: Loki-VL-proxy — Operations
